text.content:
Mission Setup Time: 20 Minutes.

The Afterlife is a fully functional establishment! All that's left are some loose ends that the owner wants shored up - certain things that are being required of her. First in line are a cleanup crew, for keeping things… discrete.

Skeletons In The Closet

Friend Opportunity: Bones Malone

There are many strange characters in Night City, but none stranger than the gruesome, skeletally-styled Mr. Malone. He's attracted to - and tends to attract - other extreme individuals, and occasionally, his friend is allowed to miscount their dice results…

Enemy Opportunity - NCPD Sergeant

The NCPD is no stranger to corruption, but the sergeant that's "supervising" Bones and his boys is the worst of the worst. Can be suitably replaced by any enemies with ties to the NCPD or its corruption.

Rep Opportunity: Rattle Em, Boys!

Take a risk to help Bones leave the NCPD a message they'll never forget.

Gig Payout

500eb + 3 Eagles per player, with a bonus of 150eb + 2 Eagles if the party finds what created the info blackout about the operation.

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Rogue Players

From here on, Rogue's keeping things pretty close to the chest, and she's got a final set of errands for her top team to handle.

Beat 2: Spooks

Rogue knows what she wants and knows what the NCPD is doing, but doesn't quite know where. Follow some of the cops to find where their cover-ups are being perpetrated.

Beat 3: The Morgue

Getting inside the NCPD sublevel might just end up being the easy part.

Beat 4: Bone To Pick

Complications emerge.

Beat 5: The Bottom Level

The players return to the Afterlife and Rogue finally introduces them to its lowest level.

This beat uses the @Scene[fqgvewCqt8z94mll]{The 7th Circle Bar & Casino} scene, as it's usually appeared until now - populate it at your discretion (with the Owner being placed in the blue room) and then read the following.

Spooky Scary

It's midnight, and you're on the job again. The Owner's text to you didn't tell you much, just to be discrete and meet her in the blue room in the 7th Circle - soundproof and regularly checked for bugs. Whatever you're going to talk about, she doesn't want word getting out.

Downstairs, the club is busy and nobody notices you enter except for Silvers at the door. The crowd is thick around you, melting across the floor in a mass of bodies and alcohol. They part around the doorwoman as she escorts you to the back of the club, and punches in a special code at the locked door of the blue room. She doesn't say a word, just holds the door for you as you make your way inside.

Sitting on one of the room's plush sofas is the Owner. Behind you, the door seals with a hiss, and the lights go down until you can only see shadows where your friends' faces should be. The Owner's cybereyes glow in the darkness, and the holographic table in the center of the room glows dimly to match.

"Get comfortable," she says. "Let's talk."

With the Afterlife's customer-facing services online, the Owner has shifted focus to the final pieces of her kingdom. The first of these is a means of keeping her affairs discrete, even when they involve bodies hitting the floor.

  • The Owner already has a cleanup crew in mind: a cyberpsycho named Bones Malone and his boys, the Rattlers.

    • Bones is a skeletally-styled former mafioso and classic gangster type with NightCorp connections.

    • His gang are a group of posers, essentially - freaky odds and ends that Bones has attracted over the years who stay close for mutual safety from NC's apex predators.

  • Bones was once responsible for cleaning up NightCorp's messes, when boardroom discussions went the wrong direction or a street gang didn't play ball.

    • After awhile, he realized he had a lot more power in secrets than he was being paid for, and NightCorp decided to contain the situation in the most brutal fashion possible.

    • The NCPD arrested Bones and his boys, and they were supposed to be executed. Instead, the NCPD's own cleanup crew decided to appropriate Bones' services for themselves, and they've been locked up in a basement somewhere in Night City ever since.

  • While the Owner knows for certain that the NCPD has Bones, she doesn't know where he is - the means and method the NCPD use for cleaning up their messes and keeping out of the media's eye is one of the most closely guarded secrets in the city.

    • This secret is itself anomalous - the Owner doesn't like not knowing things, especially when she doesn't know why they're so hard to figure out.

      • (Note: the NCPD sergeant in charge of the operation is an Arasaka deep-cover operative using the position to gather secrets and build a power base in the city - this can be revealed to your players later, in beat 3).

    • The mystery behind this secret is also why the party is meeting in the blue room instead of Rogue's office or somewhere similar.

  • That said, once found, she's certain she can get Bones and the Rattlers to play along; given what they know and their status as a psychogang, she's certain she can convince them to work by offering psychosis treatments, relative freedom, and the opportunity to hit back at Night Corp and the NCPD.

The party's job: to find Bones and his crew and free them from NCPD custody, which is itself going to be none-too-easy. The Owner is also offering bonuses if the party can figure out why and how the location of Bones and his boys were kept so secret.

She has an initial lead for the party to follow - if the party's looking for disposal services, all they need to do is follow the trail of bodies. When players are ready to get started, proceed to beat 2.

This beat is primarily Theater Of The Mind, but also uses the @Scene[jbMDq28Zr7dI96Oj]{Glen Apartment High-Rise Environment}. To begin, switch to it, then read the following:

Tracking The Trackers

As you leave the Blue Room and the sounds of the 7th Circle return, you reflect on the Owner's plan. It isn't exactly a masterstroke of intelligence, but sometimes the conventional solutions are all that's needed.

While you were talking, some Afterlife staff procured an NCPD radio, giving you the ability to tap into encrypted frequencies reserved for police deployments. Given how universally hated the NCPD is, this isn't something just anyone could get; the encryption is changed frequently, along with the codes used. Virtual intelligences on either end of the transmission are used to both decrypt and decode the reports. This way, the NCPD can't be ambushed or tracked… until today.

Sorta.

The radio's decryption program is still running, so that's the hard part done. The decoding program, though, is tied to officer agents - too risky to try and replicate, and everyone knows that the NCPD can't be paid off by anyone that makes less than 8 figures. Corruption is punished with prison time, and cops don't last long in jail.

You'll have to figure that part out yourself. Hopefully you're good with cryptography.

There are 3 ways to decode the transmissions of the NCPD radio:

  • A DV17 Cryptography check (auto-passed if the user has 4 or more ranks)

  • A DV21 Electronics/Security check (lowered to DV17 if the user has 6 or more ranks)

  • Pay a cryptographer: there's all sorts of edgerunners in the Afterlife, nowadays - paying someone to do it for you will cost 400eb… or 2 Eagles.

Once the transmission has been decoded, read on.

The Owner's instructions were simple - follow the bodies. It never makes the news, but the NCPD shoots people that it isn't supposed to all the time. Sometimes it's gangers or no-name civillians, and those bodies are left where they land. But sometimes it's the people the NCPD is actually supposed to care about - execs, their team members, diplomats - and sometimes its the people NightCorp wants dead.

In those cases, according to the Owner, Bones and the Rattlers will be on the scene in minutes, clean up, and then disappear without a trace. The NCPD keeps them pretty busy - the party just needs to wait for the right code, then follow Bones and his boys home.

And sure enough, within the hour the call goes out, and you're on the move.

The cover-up that the party intercepts is being held in the Glen. The patrol cars are already gone by the time Bones and his crew get there. Building description:

  • The target building is a 15-story apartment block, all glossy windows and red brick, with a ritzy-looking penthouse on top. The call was for a break-in on the 13th story. If there were patrol cars, they're gone by the time you arrive. There's an underground parking garage which provides the primary building access - at this time of night, it's been secured by a grate. There's also the front lobby, though those doors are locked.

  • The Owner instructs the party to tail the crew, not grab them on the job - at least a couple members are being kept "at home," wherever the crew returns to.

Tracking the cleanup

Bones and his crew have arrived via an unmarked black van, which is currently parked in a shadowy corner of the parking garage, close to the elevators. It takes them about 30 minutes to clean up the scene, and the party's arrival time is variable.

  • If the party drove themselves, a DV15 drive land vehicle check is required - auto-passed if the driver has 4 or more ranks in the skill. On failure, the party arrives about 15 minutes after Bones did - on success, they arrive 2 minutes after Bones.

  • If the party takes a combat cab, it always arrives 10 minutes after Bones.

  • If the party takes the NCART or other public transit, they arrive 20 minutes after Bones.

Getting into the building

This be done via the parking garage or the lobby, both of which are professionally secure against intrusion. Defeating either requires one without being detected in the process requires one of the following:

  • A DV23 Basic Tech check

  • A DV19 Electronics/Security check

  • A DV17 Pick Lock check

  • A DV13 Interface check (if using @UUID[JournalEntry.Em8PtvZMfXHVHmr5]{1b. Netrunning: Rebooted} - otherwise, net infrastructure is at GM discretion.)

  • If failed by 5 or more, the building security is tripped - Bones and his crew return to their van and the NCPD is called once again; when the all-clear is sounded, they resume their work.

Bones and his crew are wearing fedoras and rubber masks to cover their skeletal borgware faces and heads, as well as long coats and gloves. They specialize in stealth - nobody sees or hears them coming or going - and the bodies from the apartment they're cleaning up are transported out in rolled rugs or carpet sections, which are being "taken for cleaning" - a plausible cover story.

The crew of 4 is being escorted by a pair of @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.yK8PnIHBoxz5wfqg]{NCPD SWAT Officer}s, who are able to detonate bomb chips in the crew's heads at any time, killing them instantly.

The Cleanup

The cleanup is of an exec's apartment where a false break-in was reported. The NCPD was called when security was accidentally tripped, and automated defenses fired at the cops. The police responded by mistakenly assuming the exec and her small team were robbers; when two were killed by accident, they executed the exec herself to cover up the mistake.

Once the cleanup is complete (30 minutes), the van leaves via the parking garage exit and heads for the NCPD Morgue, which is an unremarkable basement sublevel in New Westbrook.

  • If players never identified Bones' vehicle, it leaves the garage at the appropriate time but they won't recognize it. In the time they stake out the building, two other vehicles also leave:

    • One is a black SUV with tinted windows - it's carrying a NightCorp official, who's headed to the Badlands for a discrete meeting with a Militech executive.

    • The other is a beat up 4-door sedan with armor plated everything. Its driver is a gig worker for ManyEats, a food delivery service, whose next delivery is in the Old Combat Zone. Someone had the munchies.

  • Tracking the van without being seen can be done via Combat Cab or Tracking skill.

    • If a combat cab is taken, roll a d10; on a result of 5 or less, the van realizes it's being tailed and vanishes into the Old City Center to lay low. See "if discovered" below.

    • If the party is driving itself, someone in the party must make a DV19 Tracking skill check, gaining bonuses as follows:

      • +2 if the vehicle's driver has 4 or more points in Drive Land Vehicle

      • +2 if the vehicle's driver has 4 or more points in Stealth

    • If the tracking check is failed by 4 or less, the party loses track of the van but it doesn't realize it's being tailed, and the party can start over. If failed by 5 or more, the van realizes it's being tracked and vanishes into the Old City Center to lay low. See "if discovered" below.

If Discovered

If Bones and the crew realize they're being tracked, they'll rabbit, disappearing into the Old City Center. Following them is a moot point - Bones knows the area and the party doesn't, plus, at least some of the crew is "at home" and the van won't be returning there anytime soon.

If the van is lost, its next appearance is as follows:

  • If the van didn't realize it was tailed, its next job is in 1 hour.

  • The first time the van realizes it was being tracked, the next job is in 1 day - the party will have to redo the cryptography check.

  • The second time the van realizes it was being tracked, the next job is in 5 days - the van is replaced by a groundcar of GM discretion, and the party will have to redo the cryptography check.

  • After that point, the van reappears after 1 month - the party will have to source a new scanner (the decryption measures on the one the Owner acquired are out of date) the van is replaced by a groundcar of GM discretion,and the party will have to redo the cryptography check.

Once the van has been successfully tracked to New Westbrook, proceed to beat 3.

This beat takes place at the @Scene[0YzOG7RQWv3nbC8m]{Discreet Morgue}. To begin:

  • Place a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.skUkbMc9rOygWl4o]{Generic Van} in the garage, as though it backed in.

  • Place 1*playercount @Actor[97RmZwmbbaGBX6RE]{Black Ops Soldiers} around the Morgue - patrolling the hallways, watching Bones' crew, whatever.

    • Have NCPD backup ready in the wings if things go loud - the black ops soldiers will still call them!

  • Place 1 @Actor[h2URI3iK93xB1YYU]{Arasaka Mastermind} in the office at the south side of the scene.

    • Update the mastermind's name, token name and token art to match the Black Ops soldiers. They also have a copy of the soldiers' assault rifle.

    • Although there's an obvious skill difference in combat, the mastermind is physically identical to the rest of the squad - they recognize the mastermind's voice over radio, but there are no markings to indicate command.

  • Place @Actor[f6JZ9wdvWEzo10lp]{Bones Malone, Afterlife Cleanup Crew} and 2 @Actor[GvlJENm86jrBsZck]{Rattlers (Bones' Gang)} in the garage next to the van.

    • Place 2 more Rattlers in the morgue itself, and 2 more in the furnace room.

  • Populate the map with @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.ZZKdjSmBDqF0RJOT]{RatSec Security Cameras}:

    • There's one above the garage door, one covering the outside doorway, one at either end of the long, north-south hallway at the center of the map, one in the office, and one each in the furnace room and morgue.

  • Place the party on the left side of the map, somewhere.

  • Activate the scene

Then read the following…

Meanwhile In New Westbrook

The building the van pulls intop is unremarkable - a 6 story apartment block in New Westbrook, unremarkable in every way. A 2-car garage opens in the basement level to receive it, then closes immediately afterwards. There are no signs of any other activity.

The building itself is nothing to look at - aside from the ramp down to the garage, there's a street stair access. The upstairs has a few windows illuminated, suggesting its occupied - given how overcrowded buildings like this are, that's not exactly surprising.

The street is otherwise empty.

If the cleanup crew arrived here, it's safe to assume they don't know the party is nearby. While there are cameras - they're linked onto a single architecture and being watched by someone in the office timing their sightlines - the area is likely on low alert.

Getting in is pretty easy - blowing either door would likely be simple, if noisy. Otherwise, the garage door can be lifted with an athletics check, and the lock on the side door can be picked or hacked, all at a DV19 (raised to 20 if the person doing so is attempting to dodge the field of view of the cameras in the process).

Once inside, the guards are patrolling, but it's generally another night on the job for them - aside from the covert Arasaka operative in the office that's always vigilant, most of the guards won't be expecting the unexpected and can be lured around the base to avoid attracting attention.

The party's goal is pretty simple - make contact with Bones and get him and his crew out.

Liberating Bones & The Rattlers

Bones and his boys will be generally agreeable to the party, but will take orders from any of the NCPD soldiers if given them. The reason for this is simple - the rattlers each have a pretty big bomb in their head.

  • The bombs can be disarmed with a DV15 Demolitions, DV17 Electronics/Security, or DV17 Cyberware check.

    • Once a check has been succeeded twice, attempting it for any other rattler auto-passes.

    • If a check is failed by 5 or more, the bomb detonates, killing the person equipped with it instantly and dealing 3d6 damage to anyone directly adjacent to the poor soul as they expire.

  • There's a bit of a complication - if the NCPD realize that Bones has told anyone about the bombs, they'll kill him and his crew instantly, considering it better to destroy the asset than risk them leaking secrets.

    • This is likewise true if the Rattlers manage to get away and the NCPD realizes they're gone - dead instantly. For this reason, they will not leave with the party until the chips have been disarmed unless they have no other choice.

    • The NCPD are monitoring vitals, and a basic Virtual Intelligence in the Rattlers' neuralware is also monitoring what they're saying, triggering the bomb if the Rattlers drop too obvious a hint.

Information on these bombs can be found written in an offline journal kept on the computer in the morgue, which is otherwise used to track equipment expenses and material requisitions. They can also be disarmed by hacking the Arasaka Mastermind's personal terminal - see the next heading.

Info On Arasaka

There is an info blackout perpetuated by the Arasaka Mastermind surrounding the Rattlers, and the Owner wants information on who's the cause and why. This info can be found on the Mastermind's computer (or a datachip inside their cerebrum), requiring a DV24 Electronics/Security or DV14 Interface check to crack open and access.

  • If the party can't get the data open, taking a hard drive or the Mastermind's data chip to the Owner will also suffice - they'll only receive 2/3rds the bonus, though, and only after an in-game week has passed, to reflect the Owner's time and expense in cracking the material.

As for the conspiracy itself, well…

  • The Mastermind's mission is simple - infiltrate Night City and gain leverage on NightCorp.

    • During the Red Decades, no Arasaka operations are permitted within NC, but Arasaka mostly ignores this.

    • If their covert operations are found, Night Corp contracts with Militech to help kick them out with extreme prejudice.

  • Posing as an independent Fixer, the Mastermind infiltrated the NCPD as a contract worker and was placed in charge of their cleanup division after stellar work performance.

    • From there, the Mastermind has been slowly amassing a large pile of NightCorp secrets for Arasaka's benefit, while also making sure nobody knows about the operation.

    • Talk about putting the fox in charge of the henhouse!

  • Ironically, the Mastermind also saved Bones' life as a result, having been the one to give the order to take Bones home instead of executing him and his gang as ordered, and maintaining plausible deniability about the action towards NightCorp.

    • Hence, at least in part, the reason for the info blackout.

  • Note that while NightCorp and the NCPD really don't want their dirty laundry plastered all over the net, neither are they the type of corporation to play ball with extortionists - if the party threatens to leak the information, NightCorp is big enough to place a sizeable bounty on their heads, not to mention deep-sixing any media foolish enough to publish the stuff.

    • The ruthlessness of NightCorp would be common knowledge to most Night City locals, not requiring a check - it's well known that its current membership is largely comprised of "old money" organized crime members like the Yakuza, Mafia, Mob, etc., who've "gone legit" through the corp but haven't lost their ruthless edge.

A Message They'll Never Forget

Bones and his boys have been kept captive by the NCPD for well over a year, now, and they're thirsty for revenge.

  • The method of that revenge is simple - they want to take the Mastermind alive, torture them to death, then string their mutilated remains up on the US 16 North bridge, between Watson and Little Europe…

    • …with a pair of soiled underpants hanging around their neck…

    • …while sending the contents of the agent's head to every Media in the city, still-decrpyted or otherwise - literally hanging NightCorp's dirty laundry out to dry.

    • Hey, Bones is a cyberpsycho - what did you expect?

  • Bones can be talked down from the torture and mutilation (DV17 Persuasion), and even from killing the operative - if the party finds out the Mastermind is Arasaka-affiliated, Bones will even prefer stringing the operative up alive for further humiliation and eventual grisly demise via Arasaka cleanup crew or Militech hit squad.

    • But the core mission is the same: one spy, one bridge, one pair of dirty underpants, and the contents of the operation sent to every Media from Pacifica to Sacramento.

Getting it done earns the party a personal rep, and the player who advocates the hardest for the goal will earn Bones' friendship.

Running The Escape

Maybe things will get hot, or maybe the party has somehow found a miraculous way to get Bones and his gang free without attracting attention. In either case, once the situation escalates, proceed to beat 4.

This beat is a continuation of Beat 3, but depending on your outcome, may necessitate further prep (outlined further in the scene). The beat begins when the Rattlers are about to leave the premises, or the NCPD attacks the party. There are a few disparate paths, here, so pick the pieces that suit your current situation the best.

Note that none of these scenarios account for a stop off at the north bridge to string up the Mastermind - that'll have to be all you.

Sound The Alarm!

If the party is discovered, the NCPD will not immediately call in reinforcements. They will eventually (we'll get to that) but first and foremost the Mastermind is interested in who the party are, who sent them, and how they found out about the operation in the first place.

  • When combat begins, the NCPD will seek to kill upwards of half the party - but corner and force surrender from the other half, by force if necessary.

  • Once the party has surrendered, the Mastermind (probably with the help of Bones) will interrogate surviving members for information, requiring Resist Drugs/Torture checks against the Mastermind's Technical skill.

    • Each check inflicts 1/3rd of the party member's health in damage, regardless of pass/fail (minimum 3 damage).

    • If a party member confesses, they are killed and their body incinerated, leaving no trace.

    • If a party member doesn't confess, they eventually die from the torture.

Generally speaking, this is not a good position to be in and has no defined ways out. The Owner might put out feelers to find what happened to the party eventually, or send assistance if the party can somehow signal her, but both are longshots for rescue. Generally, this is a failstate - the party dies, the job goes uncompleted.

It's The Heat!

If the party gets into a fight with the NCPD operation, Bones and his crew will be forced to fight on pain of death (remember those bombs in Beat 3?) and cheerfully assist their captors, viewing the party as just a bunch of gonk edgerunners until proven otherwise.

If the NCPD loses control of the situation (read: the Mastermind judges that they'll no longer be able to try and capture the party), they will call for reinforcements from the NCPD proper.

  • Skip tier 2 - it's straight to tier 3.

  • The NCPD force inside the morgue will try to hold out for reinforcements, and remember those bombs (maybe detonate one of Bones' gang to make an example and show that they exist) - getting Bones and his gang out without disarming them first isn't an option.

If the NCPD arrives, they will demand the party's surrender.

  • If the party complies, the NCPD will have to make use of its cleanup crew rather more intimately than normal - the party will be questioned by NCPD officials and then executed.

    • Note, however, than the NCPD official version of this will take longer - the party will be arrested, booked, taken to a police station, questioned by a variety of officers, etc., and have ample time (likely a couple of days) to try to signal for help or plan an escape before top brass orders the situation "taken care of."

Get Outta There!

If Bones and his crew are de-bombed, they'll accompany the party without issue once they learn of the Owner's offer. The NCPD won't give up lightly, however - nestled inside each of the Rattlers' cyberware is a tracking device.

  • While the Rattlers know the trackers exist, they don't know where they are, and they're full borgs - an extra electronic gizmo isn't exactly anomalous.

  • They will, however, readily share the presence of the trackers themselves.

Each tracker can be found with a DV17 conceal/reveal check, and each is hidden on a different location for each Rattler. The difficulty of this check can go up rapidly - don't be afraid to apply penalties if the search is conducted while the party is under fire or sneaking!

Rather than take the Rattlers back to the Afterlife, the Owner orders the party to secure the gang in a safehouse in Old Japantown until the trackers can be removed. And that safehouse will be necessary, since you're about to go up against Night City's Apex Predators

MaxTac Incoming

If the party manages to get out of the Morgue with the Rattlers, but the NCPD knows they're leaving, the Mastermind will quickly concoct a cover story to ensure a MaxTac response to the threat.

In such cases, the NCPD will pursue the party relentlessly through the streets of Night City as the party rushes to the safehouse - the chase can be run at GM discretion, or skipped for time. The safehouse itself is located in Old Japantown, but getting there necessitates some extra prep, as follows:

  • Above the @Scene[xgqNYCRUpTKoFfIG]{Safe House}, the entrance is located in the @Scene[fhWZB3DRDSvN2Gue]{Old Japantown Marketplace} scene.

    • See the ladder next to the elevator, outside the warehouse and next to the parking lot? It's there.

    • Once they've made it down the ladder, the party is safe - a maze of tunnels greets them and the tracking devices lose effectiveness underground.

    • Getting there, though, is a pain.

  • As the party circles closer to the neighborhood, the NCPD sets up on the scene. Place a Tier 3 Perimeter Control squad in the parking lot next to the ladder, along with the troop transport for Tier 4.

    • Place the troops accompanying the Tier 4 response around the scene - they've been directed to search for the party.

  • Meanwhile, set up MaxTac and their A/V-4 offscreen - they're circling the scene, waiting for the borg squad to rear their heads for a strike, and will deploy in 1-2 rounds after combat is re-initiated.

If the party is seen descending the ladder, they will be followed, though not by MaxTac - they're smart enough to know not to follow a borg to their den. Instead, SWAT teams will be sent into the sewers to try and flush the party out.

  • Optionally, this can result in a final stand at the safehouse scene - but the NCPD's radios stop working once they're underground, so any fight will be isolated!

Once Bones and his crew are safely sequestered in the Safehouse, proceed to beat 5.

This scene takes place in @UUID[Scene.aNH5f8HeCSS472Gr]{Afterlife Sublevel 3.} To begin, place the Owner, the player tokens, and any surviving members of Bones' crew in the furnace room, then read the following:

Safe Returns

It's only half a relief when the Owner steps out of the elevator and into the safehouse you've occupied. Hours have passed since you were upstairs, and none of you have any idea what's going on - but poking your head out could expose your position and ruin the entire plan, leaving you to quietly wait and watch old reality TV show DVDs on the safehouse's television.

Eventually, though, the Owner arrives, flanked by her head of security. She congratulates you, and Silvers produces a specialized transponder to highlight the tracking devices in each Rattler. Some are buried deep, but the borgs don't seem to mind as she digs through their endoskeletons without much regard for precision. Just one more benefit of a pain inhibitor.

Eventually the devices are found and destroyed, and the Owner introduces herself to Bones, then invites you back to the Afterlife - it's time for the bottom floor to finally be unveiled.

Switch to the Afterlife Sublevel 3 scene.

The Basement

It's a long trip back to the Afterlife, but not a complicated one. Silvers had a route mapped in advance - by the time you're above ground again, you're deep in the Old Combat Zone, far away from any kind of NCPD jurisdiction. The local 6th Street patrols that operate in their stead barely even spare you a glance - a couple of discrete cab rides later courtesy of the Afterlife, and you're safe and sound back at the hotel.

This time, the Owner leads you down, down, down - 3 stories below ground level.

"Welcome home Bones - this will be your new operating center," she says, and flips on the lights.

The room is spacious, considering its location, and well-stocked. A pair of furnaces are glowing against one wall, along with everything a crew like Bones would need - rugs, plastic wrap, an excess of cleaning supplies, and whatever else goes into making a crime scene look like it isn't a crime scene.

Bones and his crew get acquainted with the room, admiring the state-of-the-art everything, and the Owner reminds them that they'll have free reign of the place so long as they don't cause problems - officially, they'll be joining Silvers' security team.

Then she turns back to the party, producing her usual envelopes containing your pay for a job well done. She has a couple more ideas to fill the last few empty rooms - but that'll be for later. For now, she invites you to some well-earned R&R upstairs - drinks are on her.

If the gig was completed successfully, players earn their payout, and Bones' services become available immediately - check out @UUID[JournalEntry.fJorCR9hOoEzw0WA]{09. Disposal Services} for more details. Bones and his boys are pleased to be at the Afterlife, given that it grants them considerably more freedom of movement than they've had at any point before now, and will work steadily from here-on.

The end - on to the next!

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

Good morning and good evening Night Owls, this is Nightlight, voice of the city, taking the scream out of the sheets and into your ears because let's be honest, the way our education system works, most of you probably can't read anything that doesn't say jugs on the cover. That's not a slight - with all the neuralware out there and an Agent in your pocket, who needs the skill anymore? Let's get into it.

Tonight's story is one you'll only hear here, folks - with a rash of high-level exec disappearances across the Glen and Little Europe, Network 54 isn't reporting and nobody else seems to care, leaving the job to yours truly. What makes the case unusual is the lack of responsibility - no gangs or corps have stepped up to take credit or even deny an accusation, and these aren't exactly little people disappearing, either - total net worth of the dozen or so rats missing from their cages tops a cool 200 million eddies. Body snatchers, anti-corporate vigilantes, or dirty boardroom politics gone wrong? You be the judge.

In other news, dear ol' Nightlight's been asked to publish a remission for the first time ever! Seems one Continental Brands was unhappy with last week's insinuation that their synth-sirloin kibble was more man than moo, so it's time to clear the air - Nightlight never meant to insinuate anything. Now that we're up to 4 dead whistleblowers, she's outright accusing. Let's see the kibble queen get off her throne and give a tour to one of those medias blaming her for profits over people, instead of just deflecting, hm?

Finally, I'd keep an eye on your local fixer, my little Nightcrawlers - air traffic broke records last months - the sky's filled with the megazepps bringing you everything from agents to rocket launchers, and the markets are booming - almost makes you forget about the scarcity economy. If you don't have an invitation for your local block, well, maybe it's time to take 6th Street up on that offer for extra muscle and look at getting yourself noticed. Me personally, I'm on the look out for a new set of bedsheets, something with a thread count a little higher than zero. We'll have to see how that goes.

Up next it's the Jason the Slasher with Bone To Pick - keep it cold as old bones, Night City, until tomorrow.
